data.body:
## Description

Modify tests/scripts/test-detect-stack.sh to add tests that verify detect-stack.sh performs content verification, not just file existence checks.

### Specific failing tests to write:
- test_detect_stack_python_empty_pyproject: Creates fixture with empty pyproject.toml (0 bytes), asserts output is 'unknown' (not 'python-poetry') — empty file should not be treated as valid Python project marker
- test_detect_stack_node_invalid_json: Creates fixture with package.json containing invalid JSON ('not json'), asserts output is 'unknown' (not 'node-npm')
- test_detect_stack_empty_cargo_toml: Creates fixture with empty Cargo.toml (0 bytes), asserts output is 'unknown' (not 'rust-cargo')

### Test approach
Create temp fixture dirs with malformed/empty marker files, run detect-stack.sh, assert that invalid markers are not treated as valid stack detection. Tests fail (RED) because current detect-stack.sh only checks file existence with test -f.

### .test-index RED marker
Add entry: plugins/dso/scripts/detect-stack.sh: tests/scripts/test-detect-stack.sh [test_detect_stack_python_empty_pyproject]

## File Impact
- tests/scripts/test-detect-stack.sh (modify — add CoVe tests at end)
- .test-index (modify — add RED marker)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes with RED-marked tests tolerated
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] ruff check passes
  Verify: ruff check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py tests/**/*.py 2>/dev/null; true
- [ ] At least 3 new CoVe test functions exist
  Verify: grep -c 'test_detect_stack_.*empty\|test_detect_stack_.*invalid' tests/scripts/test-detect-stack.sh | awk '{exit ($1 < 3)}'
- [ ] .test-index has RED marker for detect-stack.sh
  Verify: grep -q 'detect-stack.sh.*\[test_detect_stack' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.test-index
